content: Level select at ~8-12 levels: either a linear node path / world-map (Mario-style) or a grid/list of level cards; standard state indicators are locked (greyed/padlock), unlocked (highlighted), completed (check/stars/flag). Grouping into 2 worlds of 4 is common but optional at 8 levels — a single scrollable/one-screen map is fine. Game UI Database catalogs hundreds of examples (Stage/Level Select and World Map categories). Sources: gameuidatabase.com, Unity level-select tutorials.
